description: Fair in the sense of equitable: responsive to relevant differences in needs and circumstances rather than mechanically identical treatment. The adjective 'kolo' (equal) describes sameness in measure or standing; 'thua' asks whether an arrangement treats those affected justly.
articulatory_notes: Air passes between the tongue and upper teeth for 'th', then the lips round around 'u'. The following 'a' stays a separate unstressed syllable after stressed 'thu'.
usage_notes: The adjective stands before a noun or verb as a descriptor and predicates with 'nai'. In a noun position, the quality-noun rule gives the quality fairness.

pillars.solarpunk-values: When access or resources differ, 'thua' lets an arrangement be judged by the needs it meets rather than identical shares alone.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: In an exchange or collective decision, 'thua' separates a fair outcome from automatic equality of quantity.

semantic_domains.community: equitable treatment assessed in relation to needs and circumstances
